question.text:
Consider two strings A = "qpqri"B = "pqprqrp". Let 
x be the length of the longest common subsequence 
(not necessarily contiguous) between A and B and let 
be the number of such longest common subsequences 
between A and B.Then x + 10 y = _____.

solution.text:
1. "qpqr" 

2. "qprr" 

3. "pqrr" 

Length = 4 = x 

Number of LCS = 3 = y 

x + 10y = 4 + 10 \times 3 = 34.

question.text:
Which of the following statement(s) is / are correct 
regarding Bellman-Ford shortest path algorithm? 

 P: Always finds a negative weighted cycle, if one 
exists. 

 Q: Finds whether any negative weighted cycle is 
reachable from the source.

options.text:
Neither P nor Q 

Common data for next two questions: 
A sub-sequence of a given sequence is just the given 
sequence with some elements(possibly none or all) left out. 
We are given two sequence X[m] and Y[n] of lengths m and 
n, respectively with indexes of X and Y starting from 0.

solution.text:
The Bellman-Ford algorithm may not always find 
the negative weighted cycle. However, it finds any 
negative weighted cycle that is reachable from the 
given source.

question.text:
We wish to find the length of the longest common 
subsequence (LCS) of X[m] and Y[n] as l(m,n), 
where an incomplete recursive definition for the 
function l(i, j) to compute the length of the LCS of 
X[m] and Y[n] is given below: 

l(i, j) = 0, if either i = 0 or j = 0 

    = expr1, if i, j > 0 and x[i - 1] = Y [j - 1] 


 = expr2, if i, j > 0 and x[i - 1] \neq Y [j - 1] 

Which one of the following options is correct?

solution.text:
When the currently compared elements doesn't 
match, we have two possibilities for the LCS, one 
including Y[j] but not X[i] and including X[i] but 
not Y[j].

question.text:

programming based on the correct recursive 
definition of l(i, j) of the form given above, using an

that L[i, j] = l(i, j). 

Which one of the following statements would be 
for the recursive definition of l(i, j)?

row major order or column major order of L [M, 
N].

Common data for next two questions: 
The subset-sum problem is defined as follows. Given a set 
of n positive integers, S = {a1, a2, a3, …… ,an} and a positive 
integer W, is there a subset S whose elements sum of W? A 
dynamic program for solving this problem uses a 2-
dimensional Boolean array, X with n rows and W + 1 
columns X [i, j], 1 \le i \le n, 0 \le j \le W, is TRUE if and only if 
there is a subset of {a1, a2……, ai} whose elements sum to j.

question.text:
Which of the following is valid for 2 \le i \le n, and 
ai \le j \le W?

options.text: X [i, j] = X [i - 1, j]  X[i, j − ai]
X [i, j] = X [i − 1, j]  X[i − 1, j − ai]
X [i, j] = X [i - 1, j]  X[i, j − ai]
X [i, j] = X [i − 1, j]  X[i − 1, j − ai]

solution.text:
The right answer is B X[i, j] = X[i − 1, j]  X[i − 1, 
j − ai]. 

It is NP-complete (the subset-sum problem). 

For instance, the technique checks to see if the sum 
of the elements in the set S' is zero such that S'  S 
given the set S = {-7, -3, -2, 5, 7}. 

For instance, say if S' = {-3, -2, 5}. 

x is a two-dimensional boolean array with n rows 
and W + 1 columns in the proposed dynamic 
method, where W is a positive weight or sum. 

x[i, j]1 \le i \le n, 0 \le j \le W 

The element in the subset of S is determined by the 
ith row, and the associated weight is determined by 
the jth column. 

When 2 \le i \le n and a \le j \le W 

Let S' be a subset with weight W and a1, a2,...., ai. 

This means that X[i, j] = X[i - 1, j]  X[i - 1, j - ai].

question.text:
The subset-sum problem is defined as follows: 

Given a set S of n positive integers and a positive 
integer W, determine whether there is a subset of S 
whose elements sum to W. 

An algorithm Q solves this problem in O(nW) time. 
Which of the following statements is false?

options.text:
Q solves the subset−sum problem in 
polynomial time when the input is encoded in 
unary.

Q solves the subset sum problem is polynomial 
time when the input is encoded in binary.
The subset sum problem belongs to the class NP.

The subset sum problem in NP−hard. 

Floyd Warshall's Algorithm

solution.text:
Subset of Sum is a NP Complete problem. 

 Subset sum problem  NP 

 Subset sum problem is NP-hard problem.

question.text:
The Floyd-Warshall alorithm for all-pair shortest 
paths computation is based on

options.text:
Neither Greedy nor Divide-and-conquer nor 
0/1 Knapsack Problem

solution.text:
The Floyd-Warshall's algorithm is based on 
dynamic programming. It is used to find all the pair 
shortest path for every pair (i, j), there exists two 
possibilities for every pair: 
• 
If k is not an intermediate vertex in the shortest 
path from (i) to (j) then the value of dist[i][j] 
remains same. 
• 
If k is an intermediate vertex in the shortest path 
from (i) to (j), then the value is updated as 
dist[i][j] + dist[k].

below. Note that there is only one unit of each item.

The task is to pick a subset of these items such that 
their total weight is not more than 11 kgs and their 
total value is maximized. Moreover, no item may 
be split. The total value of items picked by an 
optimal algorithm is denoted by Vopt. A greedy 
algorithm sorts the items by their value-to-weight 
ratios in descending order and packs them greedily, 
starting from the first item in the ordered list. The 
total value of items picked by the greedy algorithm 
is denoted by Vgreedy.  

The value of Vopt - Vgreedy is _______. 

Matrix Chain Multiplication

question.text:
Let A1, A2, A3, and A4 be four matrices of 
dimensions 10 \times 5, 5 \times 20, 20 \times 10, and 10 \times 5, 
respectively. The minimum number of scaler 
multiplication required to find the product 
A1A2A3A4 using the basic matrix multiplication 
method is_________.

GATE-2011 : 2M] 

Four matrices M1, M2, M3 and M4 of dimensions p \times 
q, q \times r, r \times s and s \times t respectively can be multiplied 
in several ways with different number of total scalar 
multiplications. For example when multiplied as 
((M1 \times M2) \times (M3 \times M4)), the total number of scalar 
multiplications is pqr + rst + prt. When multiplied as 
(((M1 \times M2) \times M3) \times M4), the total number of scalar 
multiplications is pqr + prs + pst. If p = 10, q = 100, 
r = 20, s = 5 and t = 80, then the minimum number 
of scalar multiplications needed is?
